key_insight: The paper proves optimal-order H^1 error estimates for first- and second-order A-stable backward difference full discretizations of Willmore flow on closed surfaces.
engineering_relevance: This gives a rigorous accuracy guarantee for evolving-surface finite element simulations of curvature-driven surface dynamics.
method: Discretize the coupled Willmore-flow evolution system with evolving surface finite elements of degree at least 2 in space.
Use backward difference methods of order 1 or 2 in time with A-stability.
Prove stability via energy estimates, G-stability, and Nevanlinna–Odeh multiplier techniques.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports both theoretical convergence analysis and numerical experiments that illustrate and complement the error estimates.
watch_item: The guarantees rely on polynomial degree at least two and on the specific stability structure of the coupled system, so lower-order or different discretizations may not inherit the same accuracy bounds.
paper_title: Error estimates for $A$-stable backward difference full discretizations of Willmore flow of closed surfaces
